_meta.description: Central herb reference library. Single source of truth for all herb identity data. Used to enrich formula breakdowns regardless of whether an interaction is found.

herbs.tcm_cautions: Caution in cold deficiency patterns with diarrhea.
Contraindicated in yin deficiency with interior heat.
Use caution with phlegm-heat or loose stools.
Toxic — contraindicated in pregnancy. Must be prepared (zhi ban xia) before internal use.
Use cautiously in deficiency conditions. Avoid in pregnancy in large doses.
Contraindicated in yin deficiency with interior heat.
Caution in liver yin deficiency with ascending liver yang. Avoid prolonged high-dose use.
Use caution in kidney yang deficiency.
Use caution in cough with excess heat or yin deficiency.
Avoid in blood cold patterns or deficiency cold.
Contraindicated in bleeding disorders, yin deficiency with fire, or profuse menstruation.
Contraindicated in pregnancy, nursing, and menses. Not for prolonged use — injures yin.
Use caution with phlegm-damp, food stagnation, or intestinal parasites.
No significant TCM cautions.
Caution in absence of blood stasis. Incompatible with Li Lu (Veratrum).
Use caution in diarrhea or abdominal distension. Caution in early pregnancy.
Contraindicated in blood deficiency patterns without wind-damp.
Caution in heat from yin deficiency.
Use caution in blood deficiency or spasms without exterior patterns.
Use caution with frequent urination from cold deficiency.
No significant TCM cautions.
No significant TCM cautions.
Highly toxic — must be properly processed. Contraindicated in pregnancy, yin deficiency with heat, and excess heat patterns.
Prolonged use may cause edema and hypertension. Incompatible with Gan Sui, Da Ji, Yuan Hua, and Hai Zao.
Contraindicated in yin deficiency with heat or pregnancy.
Use caution with excess dampness or diarrhea.
Do not cook longer than 20 minutes — active alkaloids degrade with prolonged heat.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Avoid in cold deficiency patterns.
Contraindicated in pregnancy, yin deficiency with heat, or excessive menstrual bleeding.
Caution in yin deficiency with heat or excess heat patterns.
Contraindicated in pregnancy and excessive bleeding.
Caution in pregnancy. Contraindicated in yin deficiency.
Use caution in spleen deficiency with diarrhea.
Not for prolonged use — may injure spleen yang. Caution in cold-deficiency patterns.
Contraindicated in excess heat patterns, exterior excess, or early-stage acute infections.
Use caution in spleen and stomach deficiency cold.
Avoid excessive doses — may cause nausea and diarrhea.
Caution in coughing blood or chronic lung exhaustion.
Use caution in spleen deficiency with diarrhea.
No significant TCM cautions.
Use caution in spleen deficiency with diarrhea.
Not for prolonged use — injures spleen and stomach. Contraindicated in cold-deficiency patterns.
Avoid in phlegm-fire, dampness, food stagnation, or exterior conditions.
No significant TCM cautions.
Contraindicated in yin deficiency with heat. Avoid in exterior conditions or excess heat.
Contraindicated in cough from cold phlegm or diarrhea from spleen deficiency.
Contraindicated in pregnancy, excessive menstruation, or spleen deficiency cold.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Use minimum effective dose — verify species to avoid aristolochic acid content.
Use caution in yin deficiency with heat.
Use caution in diarrhea from spleen deficiency.
Contraindicated in pregnancy, excessive menstrual bleeding, or spermatorrhea.
Caution in deficiency cold patterns with diarrhea or frequent urination.
Contraindicated in excess heat patterns and early acute illness. Incompatible with Lai Fu Zi (radish seed).
No significant TCM cautions.
Contraindicated in yin deficiency with heat or excess heat patterns.
Use caution with excess patterns.
Use caution in difficult urination or dysuria.
Contraindicated in spleen deficiency with diarrhea or phlegm obstruction.
Avoid in yin deficiency with heat or excess heat patterns.
Contraindicated in ascending qi, fullness above, or deficiency heat patterns.
Cook 30–45 minutes before adding other herbs (min jian).
Use caution in spleen deficiency with dampness, qi stagnation, phlegm, or food stagnation.
No significant TCM cautions.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Use caution in bleeding disorders.
Use caution in blood deficiency or yin deficiency without wind.
Contraindicated in cold patterns of the spleen and stomach, diarrhea, or loss of appetite.
Use caution in fire from yin deficiency or excess heat patterns.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Incompatible with Ren Shen.
Contraindicated in excess heat patterns, early exterior conditions, or measles (rashes not yet vented).
Contraindicated in qi or blood deficiency without stagnation.
Toxic in large doses — limit to 1–3g in decoction. Incompatible with Li Lu (Veratrum).
No significant TCM cautions.
Toxic in large doses. Contraindicated in infants under 1 year. Caution in diarrhea.
Contraindicated in spleen deficiency with dampness or diarrhea. Incompatible with Li Lu.
Contraindicated in pregnancy.
No significant TCM cautions.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Caution in blood deficiency without stasis.
Caution with anticoagulant medications. Large doses may cause bleeding or seizures.
Use caution in gastric ulcer or excess heat.
Use caution in cold deficiency of kidney and spleen.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Caution in spleen deficiency.
Use caution in spleen deficiency with diarrhea.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Caution in spleen deficiency.
Use caution in spleen deficiency cold.
No significant TCM cautions.
No significant TCM cautions.
Contraindicated in Spleen and Stomach deficiency Cold patterns. Use with caution in patients without excess heat signs. Mineral-derived substance — use prepared form for internal consumption.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Contraindicated in patients without excess heat and accumulation. Do not use in Spleen and Stomach Cold deficiency. Mineral-derived substance. Do not use long term.

properties: Protects Stomach in formulas containing harsh Cold herbs
herbs.tcm_cautions: No significant TCM cautions at standard dosage. Used primarily as an adjunct herb to protect digestion in formulas with strong Cold or purging herbs.

Use with caution in Spleen and Stomach Cold deficiency or diarrhea patterns. Do not cook for extended periods — add near end of decoction to preserve volatile compounds. Use with caution in pregnancy.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Contraindicated in Spleen deficiency with diarrhea. Use with caution in patients with bleeding disorders. Animal-derived substance — verify patient suitability.

properties: Topically absorbs moisture and promotes healing
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in patients with exterior pathogenic conditions — may trap pathogens. Animal/mineral-derived substance (fossilized). No significant cautions at standard internal dosage.
Use with caution in patients with exterior pathogenic conditions. Animal-derived substance (shellfish). No significant cautions at standard dosage.
Use with caution in Yin deficiency with excess sweating or spermatorrhea. Avoid in patients with hyperactive Yang rising or exhausted fluids.

Contraindicated in diarrhea from excess Heat patterns or dysenteric conditions. Use with caution in patients with internal Heat. Toxic in large doses — do not exceed recommended dosage. Use processed form (Wei Rou Dou Kou) for internal use.
Incompatible with Wu Tou (Aconite) — do not use together. Use with caution in Cold Phlegm patterns or Spleen deficiency with diarrhea.
Use with caution in Qi deficiency or diarrhea from Cold deficiency. Avoid in patterns without Phlegm obstruction.
Use with caution in Cold Phlegm patterns or Spleen and Stomach Cold deficiency. No significant cautions at standard dosage.
Use with caution in patients with Spleen deficiency Heat. Prepared substance — contains fermented wheat, herbs, and other ingredients; verify suitability for patients with wheat sensitivity.

properties: Guides Qi to Tai Yang and Du Mai
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in patients with Yin or Blood deficiency. Strong dispersing action — avoid in deficiency patterns without exterior condition. May cause nausea in sensitive individuals.

Contraindicated in hypertension, heart disease, or insomnia. Contraindicated in spontaneous sweating from deficiency. Powerful diaphoretic — not for long-term use. Drug interactions: MAOIs, cardiac stimulants, antihypertensives.
Use with caution in Cold Lung patterns or Spleen deficiency with loose stools. Generally well tolerated at standard dosage.

Heavy and cloying — use with caution in Spleen deficiency with dampness or poor digestion. May cause nausea. Dissolve separately in warm liquid or rice wine. Animal-derived — verify patient suitability.
Use with caution in Cold patterns, Qi deficiency, or Spleen deficiency with diarrhea. Generally well tolerated at standard dosage.
Toxic in large doses — do not exceed recommended dosage. Contraindicated in Yin deficiency with heat signs or excess heat patterns. Prefer short-term use. Use with caution in pregnancy.
Contraindicated in Yin deficiency with heat signs, excess fire, or damp-heat conditions. Use with caution in patients without clear Kidney Yang deficiency pattern.
Use with caution in patterns of cold and dampness. Generally well tolerated at standard dosage.
Contraindicated in Wind Cold cough or Cold patterns. Use with caution in Spleen deficiency with loose stools.
Toxic — do not exceed recommended dosage. Contraindicated in Cold patterns or Spleen and Stomach deficiency. Not for long-term use. Use with caution in pregnancy.
Use with caution in Cold Phlegm patterns or Spleen deficiency. Prepared form only — raw Nan Xing is toxic. Verify processed form for internal use.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Use with caution in Spleen deficiency with loose stools or Cold Phlegm patterns. Incompatible with Wu Tou (Aconite).
Avoid in Qi deficiency patterns — strongly moves and descends Qi. Traditionally incompatible with Ren Shen (Ginseng) — do not combine.
Use with caution in patients with constipation or abdominal distension from excess patterns. Generally well tolerated.
Contraindicated in breastfeeding mothers — strongly inhibits lactation. Use with caution during pregnancy.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Contraindicated in patients without Blood stasis. Use with caution in patients on anticoagulant therapy — additive blood-thinning risk.
Use with caution in patients without Phlegm Heat or in Cold cough patterns. Generally well tolerated at standard dosage.
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Use with caution in patients without Blood stasis or Qi stagnation. Use with caution in patients on anticoagulant therapy.
Use with caution in Spleen deficiency without food stagnation. Avoid on empty stomach. Use with caution in patients on cardiac medications — may have additive cardiovascular effects.

Contraindicated in Cold patterns or Qi and Blood deficiency without heat signs. Animal-derived substance — verify patient suitability. Substitutes for Xi Jiao (Rhinoceros Horn) in all classical formulas.
Contraindicated in pregnancy — historically used as an abortifacient. Do not use in Cold patterns or Spleen deficiency with diarrhea. Incompatible with Wu Tou (Aconite).
Contraindicated in pregnancy. Use with caution in patients on anticoagulant therapy — may potentiate anticoagulant effects. Incompatible with clove (Ding Xiang).

properties: Warms Kidney Yang and Ming Men Fire
Warms the middle and disperses Cold
Leads fire back to its source
Encourages generation of Qi and Blood
herbs.tcm_cautions: Contraindicated in pregnancy. Contraindicated in Yin deficiency with heat signs, excess heat patterns, or hemorrhagic conditions. Use with caution in patients on anticoagulant therapy — may have additive blood-thinning effects. Distinct from Gui Zhi (cinnamon twig) — different part, stronger warming action, deeper clinical reach.

properties: Promotes Qi movement in middle Jiao
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in exterior deficiency with spontaneous sweating. Not for long-term use. Distinct from Su Zi (Perilla seed) — different part, different primary action (Su Zi descends Qi and transforms Phlegm; Zi Su Ye releases exterior).
Use with caution in Yin deficiency or Blood deficiency patterns. Avoid in patterns with excess sweating or without Wind Cold.

properties: Lifts Spleen Yang and stops diarrhea
Relieves stiff neck and upper back tension
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in patients with Cold in the Stomach or profuse sweating. Generally well tolerated at standard dosage.
Contraindicated in excess heat patterns or Yin deficiency with heat signs. Incompatible with Yu Jin (Curcuma) — classical incompatibility, do not combine. Use with caution in pregnancy.

Contraindicated in pregnancy. Use with caution in Cold Phlegm patterns or Spleen deficiency with loose stools. Incompatible with Wu Tou (Aconite). Distinct from Gua Lou Ren (seed only) and Tian Hua Fen (root) — whole fruit has broader action on chest and Phlegm.

properties: Stronger blood-moving action than whole Dang Gui
herbs.tcm_cautions: Contraindicated in pregnancy — stronger blood-moving action than whole root. Use with caution in patients on anticoagulant therapy — significant additive anticoagulant risk. Contraindicated in hemorrhagic conditions. Distinct from Dang Gui (whole root) which both tonifies and moves Blood — Wei (tail) primarily moves and breaks stasis.

properties: Moderates harsh properties of other herbs
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in patients with Dampness, Phlegm, or middle Jiao fullness. Avoid in patients with diabetes or blood sugar regulation issues — dietary sugar content. Generally used as a minor herb to moderate formula harshness.

properties: Clears Heat from the nutritive level
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in patients without Deficiency Heat patterns. Not for excess Heat conditions — use Chai Hu instead for exterior Heat. Generally well tolerated.
Contraindicated in Spleen and Stomach Cold deficiency patterns. Use with caution in patients with loose stools or diarrhea from Cold. Bitter and cold — use minimum effective dose.

properties: Clears Deficiency Heat and cools Blood
Clears Lung Heat and stops cough
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in Spleen deficiency with loose stools or Cold patterns. Avoid in exterior Wind Cold conditions.

properties: Clears Heat and resolves Fire toxicity
Cools Blood and benefits the throat
herbs.tcm_cautions: Contraindicated in patients without Heat toxin patterns. Use with caution in Spleen and Stomach Cold deficiency. Not for long-term use — bitter and cold.

properties: Clears Heat and resolves Fire toxicity
Used topically for throat and skin lesions
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in patients without Heat toxin patterns or throat inflammation. Topical use is common — internal use at standard dosage is generally well tolerated.
Use with caution in patients with Blood deficiency generating Wind — address root cause. Animal-derived substance — verify patient suitability. Generally well tolerated at standard dosage.

properties: Clears Heat and unblocks painful urinary dysfunction
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in patients without Damp Heat in the Bladder. Avoid in Kidney deficiency patterns. Not for long-term use.

properties: Clears Heat and unblocks painful urinary dysfunction
herbs.tcm_cautions: Contraindicated in pregnancy — invigorates Blood and may stimulate uterus. Use with caution in Spleen deficiency without Damp Heat. Not for long-term use.

properties: Clears Heat and releases Summer Heat
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in patients without Damp Heat or in patients with Spleen deficiency. Contraindicated in pregnancy — use cautiously. Mineral-derived substance. Not for long-term internal use.
Use with caution in patients without Damp Heat or Heart Fire. Generally very mild — well tolerated at standard dosage. Often used as a minor guiding herb.
Use with caution in patterns of excess fire or Yin deficiency with heat signs. Generally well tolerated at standard dosage.
Use with caution in patients with constipation or abdominal distension from accumulation. Avoid in excess patterns.
Use with caution in patients with constipation or in excess patterns. Generally well tolerated at standard dosage.

herbs.english: Black Cardamom Fruit / Sharp-Leaf Galangal Fruit
herbs.tcm_cautions: Contraindicated in Yin deficiency with heat signs or excess heat patterns. Use with caution in patients with hemorrhagic conditions.
Use with caution in patients with Yin deficiency Heat or constipation. Animal-derived substance — verify patient suitability. Generally well tolerated at standard dosage.

properties: Classically used for palpitations and insomnia
herbs.tcm_cautions: DISCONTINUED IN MODERN PRACTICE. Contains mercury sulfide — cumulative heavy metal toxicity with repeated use. Banned or restricted in many jurisdictions including the USA, EU, and Canada. Do not prescribe internally. Included for formula composition reference only. Modern substitutes: Hu Po (amber) or Long Gu (fossil bone) for Shen-calming action.

properties: Classically used for skin lesions and abscesses
herbs.tcm_cautions: DISCONTINUED IN MODERN PRACTICE. Contains arsenic — highly toxic with cumulative heavy metal poisoning risk. Banned or restricted in most jurisdictions for internal use. Do not prescribe internally. Included for formula composition reference only. External topical use under specialist supervision only.

CITES restricted — natural musk from Moschus species is internationally controlled. Verify legal status in your jurisdiction before prescribing. Contraindicated in pregnancy — strong Blood-moving action. Modern clinical practice uses synthetic musk (artificial She Xiang) as the accepted substitute — confirm with supplier that synthetic form is being used.

properties: Extinguishes Liver Wind and stops tremors
Clears Liver Heat and brightens eyes
herbs.tcm_cautions: CITES Appendix II restricted — Saiga antelope is endangered. Verify legal status and sourcing in your jurisdiction. Accepted substitute: Shan Yang Jiao (goat horn) for non-critical formulas. Use with caution in Cold patterns or without Liver Heat and Wind.

properties: Clears Heat and resolves Fire toxicity
herbs.tcm_cautions: Contraindicated in pregnancy. Use with caution in patients without Heat toxin or excess patterns. Animal-derived substance — verify patient suitability. Natural Niu Huang is scarce — confirm authentic sourcing. Synthetic Niu Huang (Ren Gong Niu Huang) is widely used as a substitute.

properties: Stronger Shen-calming action than Long Gu
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in exterior pathogenic conditions — may trap pathogens. Animal/mineral-derived substance (fossilized). Often used interchangeably with Long Gu but with stronger sedating action. Generally well tolerated at standard dosage.

properties: Clears Liver Heat and brightens eyes
herbs.tcm_cautions: Use with caution in Cold patterns or Spleen deficiency. Animal-derived substance (mollusk). Generally well tolerated at standard dosage. Use prepared/powdered form — unprepared pearl is not bioavailable.
